source.title: Demo reviewed melena syncope case
source.citation: Local reviewed educational case file for Diagnostician demo content.
source.raw_text: De-identified educational case source assembled and reviewed for demo validation.

case.title: Melena with near syncope
teaching_points: Melena, orthostasis, elevated BUN relative to creatinine, and endoscopic ulcer findings support an upper source.
Medication history can reveal risk from NSAIDs and antiplatelet therapy.
Hemodynamic assessment matters before definitive endoscopy.

facts.label: Opening concern
facts.value: The patient reports black, tarry stools and nearly fainted when standing.

provenance.quote: Black tarry stools and near syncope when standing.

facts.label: Bleeding timeline
facts.value: He noticed dark stools for two days and felt progressively weaker this morning.

provenance.quote: Two days dark stools; weaker this morning.

facts.label: Associated symptoms
facts.value: He has mild epigastric discomfort and denies bright red blood per rectum.

provenance.quote: Mild epigastric discomfort; no bright red blood per rectum.

facts.label: Medication history
facts.value: He takes daily aspirin and frequent ibuprofen for knee pain.

facts.label: Triage vitals
facts.value: Heart rate is 116/min, blood pressure 98/60 mmHg supine and 82/54 mmHg standing, temperature 36.8 C.

provenance.quote: HR 116, BP 98/60 supine, 82/54 standing, T 36.8 C.

facts.label: Abdominal and rectal exam
facts.value: The abdomen is mildly tender in the epigastrium without peritonitis; rectal exam confirms black stool.

provenance.quote: Mild epigastric tenderness; rectal exam with black stool.

facts.label: CBC
facts.value: Hemoglobin is 8.1 g/dL, down from a documented baseline of 13.4 g/dL.

facts.label: Chemistry
facts.value: BUN is 48 mg/dL and creatinine is 1.0 mg/dL.

facts.label: Upper endoscopy
facts.value: Endoscopy finds a gastric ulcer with a visible vessel that is treated endoscopically.

provenance.quote: Gastric ulcer with visible vessel treated endoscopically.

facts.label: Dangerous differential
facts.value: A hemodynamically significant gastrointestinal blood loss process must be considered early.

provenance.quote: Melena, orthostasis, anemia, and elevated BUN support significant GI blood loss.

facts.label: Hint 1
facts.value: Use stool color, orthostatic vitals, hemoglobin change, and BUN pattern to localize the source.

provenance.quote: Melena plus BUN elevation helps localize the bleeding source.

facts.label: Final diagnosis
facts.value: Upper gastrointestinal bleed from a peptic ulcer supported by melena, orthostasis, anemia, elevated BUN, and endoscopy.

provenance.quote: Final diagnosis: upper gastrointestinal bleed due to peptic ulcer.
